rationale: 9.6yr full-stack/DevOps career at a fictional company (Stark Industries) plus Accenture with no IR, embeddings, or ranking work; AI exposure is self-learner only with no production ML evidence.

6.8yr production ML career at Uber (LTR ranking layer, relevance labeling pipeline) and Flipkart (sentence-transformer recommendation at 10M+ users) with expert Elasticsearch (73mo), Qdrant (74mo), and OpenSearch skills and a Stanford M.Tech — missing only explicit NDCG/MAP eval framework ownership.

9.9yr career entirely at IT services firms (Accenture, HCL, Mphasis) and fictional Wayne Enterprises doing QA/Java/mobile work; LangChain and Computer Vision skills signal framework enthusiasm and wrong domain, not production IR/search experience.

Only 2.7yr of experience limited to frontend (Stark Industries) and mobile dev (Infosys IT services), with QLoRA/Feature Engineering skill claims inconsistent with that career trajectory and no evidence of IR, search, or ranking work.

Single role at TCS doing marketing/demand-generation work described under a Mechanical Engineer title, with ASR as the top skill — entirely wrong domain with no ML engineering, IR, or production software development.

4.3yr career entirely at IT services firms (Wipro, Accenture) in Android mobile and QA roles with self-learner AI exposure only; MLflow skill is relevant but no production ML, IR, or ranking experience exists.

11.7yr non-technical career spanning marketing, graphic design, operations management, and content writing at Wipro, TCS, and fictional Wayne Enterprises — zero engineering or ML depth despite an M.S. ML credential from a tier_4 institution.
